currentState: Mentor and prince locked in the oldest argument between them: Arthas treats conviction as virtue, and Uther recognizes the disaster hiding inside that certainty.
powerDynamic: Arthas has immediate command of the army and the momentum of the room. Uther has moral authority, mentorship, and the clearer read, but not the power to stop the launch once Arthas decides.

physicalDynamics.pattern: Uther stands opposite Arthas at the command table, the old teacher literally braced against the same map the prince is already trying to seize with his hands.
Outside the tent their dynamic shifts from formal command argument to tired truth-telling beside a column already in motion.

nameUsage.arthasCallsUther: Bare first-name address that mixes military hierarchy with old tutelage.
nameUsage.utherCallsArthas: Uses Arthas’s name as a mentor trying, and failing, to brake a chosen course.

history.event: Sylvanas’s briefing in chapter 11 gives Uther a new battlefield to name, but the underlying flaw he sees in Arthas is clearly older than this room.
Uther openly warns Arthas not to confuse motion with sufficient understanding, and Arthas rides anyway.

trajectory: Old mentor bond -> command disagreement under plague pressure.
